text: لَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا إِنَّهُ رُبَّمَا اِشْتَبَهَ عَلَيْنَا اَلْوَقْتُ فِي يَوْمِ غَيْمٍ فَقَالَ «تَعْرِفُ هَذِهِ اَلطُّيُورَ اَلَّتِي تَكُونُ عِنْدَكُمْ بِالْعِرَاقِ يُقَالُ لَهَا اَلدُّيُوكُ» فَقَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ «إِذَا اِرْتَفَعَتْ أَصْوَاتُهَا وَ تَجَاوَبَتْ فَعِنْدَ ذَلِكَ فَصَلِّ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.669 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) Al-Farra' that a man from among their companions said to him: "Sometimes the time of prayer becomes unclear for us on a cloudy day."
The Imam said: "Do you know these birds that you have in Iraq, which are called roosters (duyook)?"
The man replied: "Yes."
The Imam said: "When their voices rise and they begin to respond to each other, then at that time, pray."
